title: Engineering flexible machine learning systems by traversing functionally invariant paths
methods: geodesic path traversal in weight space
representation diversity and coherence scoring for ensembles

data_scope.cifar100: 50,000 train + 10,000 test images, 100 classes, split into 5-10 sequential subtasks
data_scope.glue_tasks: 9 NLU tasks: QQP, SST-2, CoLA, RTE, MNLI, MRPC, QNLI, STS-B
data_scope.bert_pretraining: 45 GB Wikipedia English data, 110M parameters, 12 transformer blocks
data_scope.bert_submanifold: 300 BERT models generated via FIP composition (CL + sparsification)
data_scope.model_sizes: ViT-B 86M, ViT-H 632M, BERT 110M, DeIT-B 86M, VGG16 138M parameters
data_scope.compute: NVIDIA RTX2060 (6GB), RTX3090 (24GB), A100; ~3.5h per SplitCIFAR subtask

core_conclusion: Standard gradient descent yields a single fixed weight configuration prone to catastrophic forgetting upon adaptation. The FIP framework treats neural network weight space as a Riemannian manifold and constructs geodesic paths (functionally invariant paths) that preserve a network's input-output behavior while navigating toward secondary objectives — new tasks, sparsification, or adversarial diversity. Applied to BERT, ViT, DeIT, and CNNs on academic hardware, FIP matches or exceeds state-of-the-art methods on continual learning (91.2% ViT-B on 5 SplitCIFAR tasks), network sparsification (80% weight removal on ImageNet1K with minimal accuracy loss), and adversarial robustness (55.6% vs. 35% for independently trained ensembles).
why_it_matters: Unifies continual learning, sparsification, and adversarial robustness under a single geometric framework; shifts paradigm from single-point networks to path-connected submanifolds; scales to 600M-parameter transformers on consumer GPUs; mathematically grounds previously heuristic adaptation methods (LoRA, EWC, OGD); demonstrates non-Abelian composition of model operations as a new conceptual tool; open MIT-licensed code.
why_nature: Theory-first foundational contribution connecting differential geometry to modern ML; cross-architecture generality spanning CNNs and transformers; multi-disciplinary argument drawing on neuroscience (neural drift), physics (Riemannian geometry), and random-matrix theory (Marchenko-Pastur law); non-Abelian model transformations as a novel conceptual lens; signature Nature Machine Intelligence content.
added_value_to_my_research: FIP continual adaptation for geospatial foundation models fine-tuned across heterogeneous urban contexts without forgetting prior spatial calibration
FIP sparsification to compress spatial prediction models (MGWR, accessibility GNNs) for low-resource/edge deployment in public health settings
FIP adversarial ensembles adapted to spatial distributional shift for robust out-of-distribution spatial prediction with principled uncertainty bands
Non-Abelian FIP composition to optimize modality incorporation order in multi-source GeoAI fusion pipelines (remote sensing, POI, mobile phone, social media)
Riemannian weight-space metric as a physics-informed spatial conservation constraint for urban accessibility GNNs in 2SFCA/R2SFCA modeling

related.title: Loss of plasticity in deep continual learning
related.relation: directly addresses catastrophic forgetting and plasticity loss in continual learning — the core problem FIP solves

related.title: Three types of incremental learning
related.relation: taxonomizes incremental/continual learning paradigms that FIP is benchmarked against

related.title: Continual learning of context-dependent processing in neural networks
related.relation: early continual learning framework for deep networks, directly in the same problem space as FIP

related.title: Random sketch learning for deep neural networks in edge computing
related.relation: complementary network compression strategy for deployment-constrained environments, related to FIP sparsification

related.title: Towards quantum enhanced adversarial robustness in machine learning
related.relation: adversarial robustness of ML models — the third application domain of FIP

related.title: Foundation model of neural activity predicts response to new stimulus types
related.relation: foundation model generalization and adaptation to new tasks — shares the broader paradigm of flexible neural network adaptation

figures.caption: Differential geometric framework for constructing FIPs in weight space — illustrates the isoperformance submanifold, the Riemannian metric construction, the FIP optimization step, and the path-sampling algorithm.
Vision transformers (ViT-B and ViT-H) learn 5 sequential CIFAR-100 tasks without catastrophic forgetting via FIP (91.2%/89.3%), versus complete forgetting with LoRA (Task 1 accuracy collapses to 0%).
BERT learns IMDb sentiment (0%→92%) while maintaining Yelp accuracy (70%) by traversing an FIP; conventional fine-tuning drops Yelp to 17% within 30 training steps.
FIP sparsifies DeIT-B on ImageNet1K across 0–80% sparsity (80.22% accuracy at 40%, vs. SViT 81.56%) and sparsifies BERT across all nine GLUE tasks.

FIP ensemble of 10 networks achieves 55.6% adversarial accuracy on CIFAR-10 (PGD attack), outperforming DeepNet (35%), ADP (43.8%), and FGE (41.7%) ensembles through higher weight diversity and lower coherence with the attack surrogate.

Non-Abelian iterative transformation of BERT: composing CL and sparsification operations in different orders generates 300 distinct BERT models; submanifold queried by perplexity on IMDb and WikiText.
